how to convert x.Items.GroupBy(y => y.Items.Select(z => z.User.Building)) to list<string>

To convert the result of `x.Items.GroupBy(y => y.Items.Select(z => z.User.Building))` into a `List<string>`, you can modify the LINQ query and use `SelectMany()` to flatten the resulting groups. Here's an example:

```csharp
var groups = x.Items
    .GroupBy(y => y.Items.Select(z => z.User.Building));

List<string> resultList = groups
    .SelectMany(group => group.Key)
    .Distinct()
    .ToList();
```

In this code snippet, we execute the `GroupBy()` query on `x.Items` based on the result of `y.Items.Select(z => z.User.Building)`. This will create groups based on the selected property.

Next, we use `SelectMany()` to flatten the resulting groups and select all the distinct `Building` values. Finally, we call `ToList()` to convert the result into a `List<string>`.

Make sure to adjust the code according to your actual class names and properties.
after that I want to use .Select using y.Key

If you want to use `.Select()` to operate on `y.Key` after grouping, you can modify the code as follows:

```csharp
var groups = x.Items
    .GroupBy(y => y.Items.Select(z => z.User.Building));

List<string> resultList = groups
    .SelectMany(group => group.Key)
    .Select(key => // Your transformation logic using y.Key goes here)
    .ToList();
```

In this code, after grouping `x.Items` by `y => y.Items.Select(z => z.User.Building)`, we can then use `.Select()` on `group.Key` to perform further transformations.

You need to replace `// Your transformation logic using y.Key goes here` with your own logic to transform `key`. This logic can refer to `key`, which represents `y.Key` in your original query.

For example, if you want to transform each `key` to uppercase, you can use `.ToUpper()`:

```csharp
var groups = x.Items
    .GroupBy(y => y.Items.Select(z => z.User.Building));

List<string> resultList = groups
    .SelectMany(group => group.Key)
    .Select(key => key.ToUpper())
    .ToList();
```

Adjust the code inside the `.Select()` according to your specific transformation requirements.
